content:
As @5DN1L will tell you, please study the formatting syntax on this page: https://www.codingame.com/playgrounds/40701/help-center/statement, and format your statement properly as described in the points below.

In my opinion, this is not suitable for a clash. Players who can't deduce the invariant will not be able to start on the clash, let alone craft a solution. This problem should be converted into a puzzle (of Easy-Medium difficulty) instead.

Edit: Just reviewed your puzzle contribution. Perhaps you mixed up these two problems somehow?
oops -_-, my bad, I'll fix it but currently busy for a week or so.

Your clash also has the issue that all the cases feature either grids that are already optimal (requiring 0 operations) or grids that are impossible to stabilise (-1). There are no cases where the grid can be stabilised but only after a non-zero number of operations.

By the way, there's a clash with a related task: https://www.codingame.com/contribute/view/128088362f5629be6b89f6a197a68ddf40ae03

In that clash, however, the allowed operations involve decrements rather than increments, and the grids are always reducible. This makes the task easier.
k. uhhh, let me just make this a puzzle... following on from @User123's comment
